channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Fri, 15 May 2026 16:42:36 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Imam, 54, who claimed ‘jinn powers’ jailed for life over abuse crimes against women and girls
item.description: A former East London imam, Abdul Halim Khan, has been jailed for life after being convicted of 21 sexual offences, including rape and child rape, against seven victims over a decade. He exploited his religious authority, claiming supernatural powers to manipulate and abuse vulnerable women and children. The case highlights safeguarding failures and barriers victims face when reporting abuse.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Buckingham Palace hosts historic meeting between King Charles and New Zealand’s Māori Queen
item.description: New Zealand's Māori Queen, Ngā Wai Hono i te Pō, met King Charles III at Buckingham Palace, marking a significant moment for Commonwealth ties. The meeting highlighted discussions on climate challenges, Indigenous leadership, and youth empowerment, aligning with the King's long-standing interests and the evolving role of the monarchy.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘Xi would also like to see the Hormuz Strait open’: Trump on US-China talks
item.description: US President Trump revealed Chinese President Xi Jinping desires the Strait of Hormuz reopened to stabilize global energy markets. Both leaders reportedly agreed on the necessity of this crucial shipping route's resumption. Discussions also touched upon potential increased Chinese purchases of American oil and agricultural products, alongside ongoing tensions regarding Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: "Why did the military prioritize Erika": Candace Owens claims Erika Kirk’s emotional WHCD shooting clip was strategically amplified
item.description: Candace Owens claimed the White House Military Office helped amplify a viral video of Erika Kirk emotionally leaving the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting scene saying, “I just wanna go home.” Owens alleged a leaked internal email described the clip as a powerful emotional asset. No evidence has publicly confirmed the claim, but the accusation has intensified Owens’ ongoing feud with Erika Kirk.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Who is Chud the Builder? Controversial creator Dalton Eatherly linked to viral chaos and Tennessee shooting investigation
item.description: Dalton Eatherly, known online as “Chud the Builder,” became famous for controversial livestreams, rage-bait videos, and disruptive public behavior. The influencer is now back in headlines after a courthouse shooting investigation in Clarksville, Tennessee left two people injured. The incident has sparked wider debate about internet fame, shock content, influencer culture, and how online controversy can sometimes spill into real-world situations.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: Who is Empath Chan? Controversial streamer shuts down viral death rumors with blunt response
item.description: Empath Chan responded after fake rumors about her death spread across X following a parody post claiming she died from a drug overdose. She shut down the speculation with a brief reply saying, “? I’m alive.” The controversy renewed attention on her history of ragebait content, including backlash over blackface Halloween posts and offensive videos that previously sparked widespread criticism online.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: “We no longer have free speech”: Sneako compares Chud the Builder backlash to Charlie Kirk debate after shocking Tennessee arrest case
item.description: Sneako reacted strongly after Chud the Builder’s Tennessee arrest, calling it a dangerous moment for free speech rights. Meanwhile, authorities charged Dalton Eatherly with attempted murder and other firearm-related offenses after a courthouse shooting in Clarksville. The incident has now started a heated online debate involving hate speech, violence, constitutional rights, and comparisons to Charlie Kirk.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: The $55 million blueprint: How New York’s busiest library swapped worn-out shelves for a civic living room
item.description: A significant $55 million donation revitalised the Mid-Manhattan Library, transforming it into the Stavros Niarchos Foundation Library. This renovation shifted focus from book storage to community needs, creating dynamic spaces for learning, interaction, and professional development. The redesigned library now serves as a vital, free democratic hub for ordinary New Yorkers.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The humility habit: Why Bill Gates and Warren Buffett voluntarily swapped the boardroom for a fast food counter
item.description: Top leaders like Bill Gates and Warren Buffett learned a crucial lesson by working behind a Dairy Queen counter. This hands-on experience revealed operational realities often missed by senior management, highlighting how distance from the ground floor can compromise decision-making. Leaders must actively seek firsthand understanding to avoid blind spots and improve workflows.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 5 massive pyramids still standing today: Why the largest one is not in Egypt
item.description: Ancient pyramids are often associated with Egypt. However, the largest pyramid ever built is in Mexico. The Great Pyramid of Cholula, known as Tlachihualtepetl, is a man-made mountain. It has a massive footprint, dwarfing the Great Pyramid of Giza in volume. This Mexican marvel is often overlooked, mistaken for a natural hill.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Brazilian influencer on the run after fleeing sentencing in fatal road crash case
item.description: A Brazilian influencer, Rosa Iberê Tavares Dantas, has become a fugitive after fleeing the country before beginning a three-year prison sentence for negligent homicide. She was convicted in a fatal road accident that killed a 31-year-old personal trainer. Authorities have placed her on Brazil's arrest warrant system and notified international agencies.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Why a Bill Gates-backed insect factory releases 30 million mosquitoes every week
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: “He’s texting some hot actress”: Candace Owens reacts after Brigitte Macron slap claims shock France again
item.description: Candace Owens reacted after a new book claimed Brigitte Macron slapped Emmanuel Macron over alleged messages to actress Golshifteh Farahani. The French first lady has denied the claims, while Farahani also rejected relationship rumors. The controversy comes as Owens continues fighting a defamation lawsuit filed by the French first couple over her past comments and online series.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: Yale medical school discriminated against Asian and White applicants, claims Trump administration
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian-origin doctor under probe for 'birth tourism' in San Jose: 'Foreign expectant mothers get extra service'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Streamer ChudTheBuilder charged with attempted murder after Tennessee courthouse shooting
item.description: Clarksville streamer Dalton Eatherly, also known as ChudTheBuilder, faces attempted murder charges after a violent confrontation outside a courthouse in Clarksville. The 28-year-old internet personality, previously criticized for provocative online behavior, was injured alongside another man. Both are in stable condition as authorities investigate the incident and Eatherly's controversial past.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Scientists say SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket will strike the Moon at 5,400 miles per hour
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Hidden beneath Oklahoma’s white salt flats lies a crystal found nowhere else on Earth that people can dig up for free
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Meet Grigori Perelman: The math genius who solved a 100-year-old problem and rejected a $1 million prize and the Fields Medal
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: From searching for stars to finding Wi-Fi: The failed radar experiment that changed the world
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk
